items.dialect_sql: SELECT * FROM employees;
items.label: scan_all
items.ref_sql: SELECT * FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id, name FROM employees;
items.label: scan_subset
items.ref_sql: SELECT id, name FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id = 10;
items.label: filter_eq
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id = 10

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id <> 10;
items.label: filter_neq
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id <> 10

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IN (10, 20);
items.label: filter_in
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IN (10, 20)

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary BETWEEN 80000 AND 120000;
items.label: filter_between
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary BETWEEN 80000 AND 120000

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE name LIKE 'A%';
items.label: filter_like
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE name LIKE 'A%'

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IS NULL;
items.label: filter_is_null
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IS NULL

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IS NOT NULL;
items.label: filter_is_not_null
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IS NOT NULL

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id, salary + 1000 AS bumped FROM employees;
items.label: project_arith
items.ref_sql: SELECT id, salary + 1000 AS bumped FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT name || '!' AS shouted FROM employees;
items.label: project_concat
items.ref_sql: SELECT name || '!' AS shouted FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id, CASE WHEN salary > 100000 THEN 'high' ELSE 'low' END AS tier FROM employees;
items.label: project_case
items.ref_sql: SELECT id, CASE WHEN salary > 100000 THEN 'high' ELSE 'low' END AS tier FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT e.id, d.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
items.label: join_inner
items.ref_sql: SELECT e.id, d.name FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id

items.dialect_sql: SELECT e.id, d.name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
items.label: join_left
items.ref_sql: SELECT e.id, d.name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id

items.dialect_sql: SELECT dept_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id;
items.label: agg_group_by
items.ref_sql: SELECT dept_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id

items.dialect_sql: SELECT dept_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
items.label: agg_having
items.ref_sql: SELECT dept_id, COUNT(*) AS n FROM employees GROUP BY dept_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees ORDER BY salary ASC;
items.label: order_asc
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees ORDER BY salary ASC

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees ORDER BY dept_id DESC NULLS LAST;
items.label: order_desc_nulls_last
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees ORDER BY dept_id DESC NULLS LAST

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees SAMPLE 5;
items.label: limit_only
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees LIMIT 5

items.dialect_sql: SELECT DISTINCT dept_id FROM employees;
items.label: distinct
items.ref_sql: SELECT DISTINCT dept_id FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees UNION ALL SELECT id FROM departments;
items.label: union_all
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees UNION ALL SELECT id FROM departments

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IN (SELECT id FROM departments);
items.label: subq_in
items.ref_sql: SELECT id FROM employees WHERE dept_id IN (SELECT id FROM departments)

items.dialect_sql: DEFINE high AS (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary > 100000) SELECT * FROM high;
items.label: cte_simple
items.ref_sql: WITH high AS (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE salary > 100000) SELECT * FROM high

items.dialect_sql: SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY dept_id ORDER BY salary) AS rn FROM employees;
items.label: window_row_number
items.ref_sql: SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY dept_id ORDER BY salary) AS rn FROM employees

items.dialect_sql: SELECT CAST(salary AS INT) FROM employees;
items.label: cast_int
items.ref_sql: SELECT CAST(salary AS INT) FROM employees
